office_does: A U.S. representative writes and votes on federal law and spending, conducts oversight, and provides constituent services.
race_blurb: Two-term incumbent Marie Gluesenkamp Perez faces eight challengers spanning institutional conservatism, progressive economics, civil liberties, anti-party reform, and business-focused platforms.

candidates.summary: Two-term incumbent and auto-shop owner emphasizing trades, manufacturing, rural investment, supply-side housing, abortion rights, and anti-corporate reforms.
highlights: Supports cutting red tape to build housing [S1]
Opposes corporate PAC money and introduced a congressional stock-trading ban [S1]
Supports abortion rights and rural health and infrastructure [S1]

spending.basis: Supports rural health, infrastructure, school, road, forest, and farmland investment.

reform.basis: Rejects corporate PAC money and introduced stock-trading and congressional-pay legislation.

healthcare.basis: Supports cheaper generics and rural care within existing programs.

experience.basis: Two-term incumbent with committee and legislative record.

candidates.summary: Project manager supporting progressive taxes, health-care expansion, infrastructure and social investment, ICE constraints, reduced war spending, and PAC independence.
highlights: Supports higher taxes on wealthy people and rejects corporate PAC money [S1]
Supports universal-health movement and investment [S1][S2]
Opposes wars and ICE practices [S1]

taxes.basis: Explicit higher taxes on wealthy people.

spending.basis: Broad school, infrastructure, health, broadband, and social investment.

healthcare.basis: Supports movement toward universal care but mechanism is unspecified.

candidates.summary: Therapist and former professor emphasizing immigration enforcement, traditional social positions, and a public-works housing concept.
highlights: Supports removing immigrants he describes as criminals [S1]
Opposes DEI in the military and transgender women in women's sports [S1]

immigration.basis: Supports removal of immigrants characterized as criminals while retaining others.

social.basis: Explicit traditional positions on military DEI and transgender sports.

candidates.summary: Power-industry operator centered on anti-donor, anti-two-party, anti-intervention, fiscal, and corporate-power critiques.
highlights: Argues major parties serve donors and corporations [S1][S2]
Criticizes military intervention and national-debt growth [S1]

candidates.summary: IT professional supporting a wealth tax, expanded nutrition aid, low-income advocacy, and more affordable health care.
highlights: Supports a wealth tax and expanded SNAP [S1][S2]
Supports low-income advocacy and more affordable health care [S1]

spending.basis: Supports expanded SNAP and low-income programs.

candidates.summary: State Senate Republican leader and retired Navy captain emphasizing tax and spending restraint, enforcement, defense, border security, and parental rights.
highlights: Supports lower taxes, reduced spending, law enforcement, and parental rights [S1]
Supports border security and national defense [S3]
Long state legislative and budget record [S1][S2]

spending.basis: Explicit reduced spending and waste-control platform.

defense.basis: National-defense support and retired Navy leadership.

experience.basis: Long legislative, budget, military, and business leadership record.

candidates.summary: Entrepreneur and oyster farmer emphasizing business, workforce, affordable energy, education, natural resources, and outsider pragmatism.
highlights: Supports local economies, workforce training, small businesses, and affordable energy [S1][S2]
Runs against political insiders [S1]

climate.basis: Prioritizes energy affordability with responsible-resource language.

experience.basis: Business outsider with no elected record beyond precinct service.

candidates.summary: Navy veteran emphasizing civil liberties, police and ICE accountability, surveillance limits, PAC independence, lower war spending, and AI ownership protections.
highlights: Supports police accountability and keeping ICE out of Vancouver [S1][S2]
Rejects corporate PAC money and supports reducing war spending [S1]
Supports surveillance limits and AI ownership protections [S1]

safety.basis: Police-accountability emphasis leans away from enforcement-first.

reform.basis: Rejects corporate PAC money and supports federal transparency.

immigration.basis: Explicitly supports excluding ICE from Vancouver.

tech.basis: Supports protections against AI-related ownership loss and surveillance.

candidates.summary: Retired pilot supporting lower taxes, border enforcement, business conditions, transparency, voter access, and rejection of unsupported fraud claims.
highlights: Criticizes high taxation and open borders [S1]
Supports voter access and congressional transparency [S1]

immigration.basis: Explicit open-border criticism and enforcement posture.

reform.basis: Supports transparency and voter access while rejecting unsupported fraud claims.
